name: Alerts
description: Notifications requiring attention — produced by Monitors, agent observations, or system events. Alerts have severity, an audience, and a status that progresses from open → acknowledged → resolved.

properties.description: Short headline. Example: 'Usage drop detected: Meridian Health', 'Reply requires AE response', 'HubSpot sync stalled 4h'.
info = good to know; warning = action needed soon; critical = act immediately.

What kind of alert. anomaly = unexpected pattern; threshold = a value crossed a line; reminder = scheduled check-in; escalation = requires manager attention; opportunity = positive signal worth acting on; error = system failure; handoff = agent → human transfer.

Full alert message. Should be self-contained — a reader who hasn't seen the source data should still understand the situation. Examples: 'Login frequency dropped 40% week-over-week. Renewal in 3 months.', 'Reply from VP Sales at tier-A account — handoff to AE.'
Who should see this alert. user = the operating user; team = the team channel; manager = escalation channel; agent = consumed by another operation; system = log-only.
Lifecycle. open = needs attention; acknowledged = seen; resolved = action taken; dismissed = won't act; expired = aged out without resolution.
When the alert moved from open → acknowledged.
When the alert moved from acknowledged → resolved/dismissed.
What was done. One sentence. Example: 'Booked QBR with champion for April 3', 'Sync restarted, backlog cleared in 1h'.
If this alert was raised by a Monitor, the monitor_id. Empty for ad-hoc alerts.
Value of the linked entity's key. Use memory_filter_by_property(type='alert', custom_key_value=...) to find all alerts for an entity.
Project / campaign this alert belongs to, when applicable.
Optional auto-expiration. Alerts past this time auto-transition to status='expired' if still open.
